# trocco_dbt_git_repository (Resource)

Provides a TROCCO dbt Git repository resource.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "trocco_dbt_git_repository" "example" {
  name         = "example repository"
  description  = "example repository description"
  adapter_type = "bigquery"
  dbt_version  = "1.11"
  url          = "git@github.com:example/repo.git"
  branch       = "main"
  subdirectory = "dbt/"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `adapter_type` (String) The adapter type. Must be one of `bigquery`, `snowflake`, `redshift`. Cannot be changed after creation.
- `branch` (String) The branch of the Git repository to sync from.
- `dbt_version` (String) The dbt version (e.g., `1.11`). Supported values follow what TROCCO currently allows; refer to the TROCCO documentation for the latest list.
- `name` (String) The name of the dbt Git repository.
- `url` (String) The URL of the Git repository.

### Optional

- `description` (String) The description of the dbt Git repository.
- `resource_group_id` (Number) The ID of the resource group that the dbt Git repository belongs to.
- `subdirectory` (String) The subdirectory where the dbt project is located in the Git repository.

### Read-Only

- `id` (Number) The ID of the dbt Git repository.



## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, use an [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) to import trocco_dbt_git_repository (Resource). For example:

```terraform
import {
  id = 1
  to = trocco_dbt_git_repository.example
}
```

Using the [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import):

```shell
terraform import trocco_dbt_git_repository.example <dbt_git_repository_id>
```
